summary: Static verification of rebranding MedicalApp -> MyMedicalApp.NET across 16 files. All primary spec items pass: 199 MyMedicalApp.NET occurrences in Loc.cs, AppTitle rewritten in all 7 languages, key parity 1011 per language (en/ro/fr/es/de/it/pt) - full parity, no www.MedicalApp.com or MedicalApp.com occurrences remain, SenderName = 'MyMedicalApp.NET', brace balance = 0 in all 16 files, footer mailto = contact@mymedicalapp.net, LLM prompts (Gemini/OpenAI line 774/127) still say 'MedicalApp' (protected), CAM regex [MedicalApp] preserved, namespace MedicalApp.Services intact, appsettings SenderEmail still vasilepintilie2003@gmail.com as expected. Sandbox has no dotnet SDK -- no compile test possible; runtime testing to be done by user on VS2026.

minor.issue: 'Powered by' link uses https://mymedicalapp.net (missing www.). Spec calls for www.mymedicalapp.net everywhere.
B2B patient email 'Powered by' href/label uses bare mymedicalapp.net (no www.) - inconsistent with all other URLs which use www.mymedicalapp.net.

XML doc comments on CallGeminiWithRetryAsync still describe engine labels as 'motor MedicalApp' / 'motor MedicalApp+' / 'motor MedicalApp Plus'. Actual LabelFor() runtime output correctly returns MyMedicalApp.NET variants - comment/code drift only, non user-facing.

Admin purchase-notification email SUBJECT prefix is [MedicalApp] (also DailySummaryService.cs:216 uses [MedicalApp] Rezumat zilnic). Ambiguous: if these are intended as internal admin-tag markers they can stay; if intended as brand references they should be [MyMedicalApp.NET]. Please confirm intent.
Doc comment mentions 'sugerate de MedicalApp' - comment-only, non user-facing (actual UI disclaimer text lives in Loc.cs).
XML doc comments still reference 'MedicalApp+' - comment-only, safe to leave but noted for completeness.

action_items: Decide www vs bare domain: add 'www.' to 3 hrefs (CAM Dashboard Index.cshtml:524; CamPatientEmailBuilder.cs:97 href and :99 href) OR update spec to accept apex domain. Note label text on line 99 ('mymedicalapp.net') is visible to end users.
Confirm whether email SUBJECT prefixes '[MedicalApp]' in CreditsController.cs:193 and DailySummaryService.cs:216 should remain (internal tag) or be rebranded.
Optional cleanup: sync XML doc comments in CamBatchService.cs (692-707), CamPatientEmailBuilder.cs (10), SupportedLanguagesConfig.cs (4), InterpretationResult.cs (66) to new brand for consistency.
critical_code_review_comments: Loc.cs is 7500+ lines with 7 duplicated dictionaries - long-term maintainability risk (out of scope for this iteration).
Rebrand cleanly separates brand text from protected identifiers (namespace, CAM PDF marker, LLM prompts) - good architectural discipline.
Minor: apex vs www domain inconsistency should be normalized across all links + labels.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 9 was static-only (no dotnet SDK in sandbox). All primary rebrand goals verified: 199 MyMedicalApp.NET in Loc.cs, key parity 1011 per lang, 7 AppTitle rewrites, no MedicalApp.com anywhere, SenderName updated, all protected items (namespace, [MedicalApp] regex + badge, LLM prompts, LoincSeeder folder comment) intact, brace balance 0 in all 16 modified files. Only remaining nits are 3 apex-domain hrefs missing 'www.' and doc-comment drift in a handful of files. Runtime build/UI validation must be done by user on VS2026.
